reasoning: A had the most genuinely distinct set of five: ingested sourdough arc (taste), contingent sand-floor topography where a lazy day physically moves less than a frantic one, thermal-lag walls, day-speed social audio read by crowd density, and an emergent crowd-motion pendulum outsourcing timekeeping to an uncoordinated swarm. The floor-remembers-feet and crowd-pendulum ideas escape the obvious frame while staying coherent. C is a near-equal: salt-garden day-as-solid-object read after the fact, a reversed decaying loaf, a draining-cistern wire that sings a rising glissando, and a species-relay biological clock are bold and well-built, though its distributed water-vessel idea overlaps conceptually with A's thermal touch idea. B ranks last because its five collapse into roughly two clusters (smell/taste arc in the aging bread and tasting menu, and run-your-hand-along-a-changing-surface in the texture wall and thermal handrail), and each is a single vivid sentence with little development.
